cases.title: Staff check-in within window records CheckinRecord with timestamp
preconditions: Event is InProgress with check-in window active (current time between checkinOpenAt and checkinCloseAt)
Participant has registration in Registered state with no prior valid check-in
Authenticated OrganizerStaff or OrganizerAdmin actor with check-in capability for the event
steps: Invoke checkinService.staffCheckin(eventId, { registrationId }, actorContext) at a fixed in-window time
Read returned check-in snapshot and linked registration state
cases.expected: CheckinRecord is persisted with non-null checkinAt ISO timestamp; registration transitions to CheckedIn; method indicates staff check-in

cases.title: POST checkins returns HTTP 200 with checkinAt and CheckedIn registration
preconditions: E2E fixture event in InProgress state with active check-in window
Registered participant with valid registrationId
Staff or admin JWT with scope for the event
Fresh Idempotency-Key header value
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/checkins with body { registrationId } and Authorization bearer token
Optionally GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/attendance and locate the participant row
cases.expected: Response HTTP 200 includes checkinAt timestamp, registrationState CheckedIn, participantId, and method Staff; attendance list includes the participant with matching checkinAt

cases.title: POST self-checkin records timestamp when self-service is enabled
preconditions: Event rule config enables participant self check-in
Event is InProgress with active check-in window
Authenticated participant JWT for a Registered registration with no prior check-in
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/self-checkin with participant Authorization bearer token
Read response body and participant registration state
cases.expected: Response HTTP 200 with non-null checkinAt, registrationState CheckedIn, method Self (or equivalent self-service indicator); no staff operatorId required

cases.title: Check-in window validator accepts time strictly inside open-close bounds
preconditions: Configured checkinOpenAt and checkinCloseAt with openAt < closeAt
Fixed clock now set to a time between openAt and closeAt (exclusive of boundaries if validator uses strict comparison)
steps: Call check-in window validator (e.g. isWithinCheckinWindow(openAt, closeAt, now)) with in-window now
cases.expected: Validator returns true, permitting check-in processing for AC-05 happy path

cases.title: Participant completes self check-in via UI and sees success with timestamp
preconditions: Event with self check-in enabled and window currently open
Participant logged in with Registered status for the event
Event in InProgress or equivalent state allowing check-in
steps: Navigate to event self check-in page for the registered event
Trigger check-in action (button or equivalent primary control)
Observe success confirmation and check-in status display
cases.expected: UI shows check-in succeeded with visible timestamp or time-of-check-in indicator; registration status reflects checked-in state without manual page reload beyond normal client refresh

cases.title: Organizer staff check-in console records participant with timestamp
preconditions: OrganizerStaff logged in with assigned event scope
Event InProgress with open check-in window
Target participant appears in check-in list with Registered state and eligible for check-in
steps: Open organizer check-in operations console for the event
Identify registered participant and trigger staff check-in action
Verify row updates in console and/or attendance history
cases.expected: Console shows check-in success for the participant with recorded time; participant row reflects checked-in status immediately per UI state transition rules

cases.title: Check-in at CheckinOpenAt boundary is recorded with timestamp
preconditions: Event configured with known checkinOpenAt and checkinCloseAt
Fixed clock set exactly to checkinOpenAt
Registered participant with no prior check-in
steps: Submit staff or self check-in at the boundary instant checkinOpenAt
Query CheckinRecord and registration state
cases.expected: Check-in succeeds per window policy; checkinAt is persisted and registration is CheckedIn

cases.title: Second check-in attempt for same registration is rejected after first success
preconditions: Participant already has one valid check-in recorded within the window
Registration state is CheckedIn
Check-in window still open
steps: Attempt a second check-in for the same registrationId via staff or self endpoint
Observe error response and database state
cases.expected: Request fails with stable error code CHECKIN_ALREADY_RECORDED (409 or 422); original checkinAt unchanged; no duplicate CheckinRecord created

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate check-in requests yield at most one valid record
preconditions: Registered participant with no prior check-in
Active check-in window
Two or more parallel check-in requests targeting the same registrationId
steps: Fire concurrent staffCheckin (or API POST) calls for the same registration
Count valid CheckinRecord rows and inspect registration state after all requests settle
cases.expected: Exactly one check-in succeeds with timestamp; other requests receive CHECKIN_ALREADY_RECORDED or equivalent conflict; registration ends in CheckedIn with single valid check-in invariant (INV-04) preserved

cases.title: Check-in rejected when registration is not in Registered state
preconditions: Event with active check-in window
Registration in Waitlisted, CancelledByUser, or other non-Registered terminal/non-checkinable state
Valid authenticated actor attempting check-in
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/checkins (or self-checkin) for the ineligible registrationId
cases.expected: HTTP 409 or 422 with typed domain error; no CheckinRecord created; registration state unchanged

cases.title: Check-in write and registration state transition commit atomically
preconditions: Database transaction boundary enabled for check-in module (check-in write + registration transition in one transaction per module breakdown)
Registered participant, in-window, no prior check-in
steps: Complete successful in-window check-in
Query checkin_records row and registration state in the same database session immediately after commit
cases.expected: CheckinRecord and registration CheckedIn state exist together; partial writes (record without state change or vice versa) do not occur

cases.title: Successful check-in persists audit metadata (timestamp, actor, method)
preconditions: In-window check-in about to be recorded
Staff check-in with known operatorId or self check-in with participant actor
steps: Perform successful check-in
Inspect persisted CheckinRecord and related audit metadata fields
cases.expected: Record includes checkinAt timestamp, check-in method (Staff/Self), and actor reference (operatorId for staff, participant for self) satisfying BR-13 audit trail requirement

cases.title: Idempotent check-in retry with same Idempotency-Key returns consistent snapshot
preconditions: Registered participant, active check-in window
Fixed Idempotency-Key header value for check-in write endpoint
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/checkins with Idempotency-Key K and receive success with checkinAt
Repeat POST with the same Idempotency-Key K and identical payload
cases.expected: Both responses return HTTP 200 with the same checkinAt and registrationId; only one CheckinRecord exists; no duplicate state transition side effects

cases.title: Flow A lifecycle check-in step stores timestamp after registration
preconditions: E2E Flow A fixture: event progressed through publish, open-registration, and participant Registered
Event started (InProgress) with check-in window open
Participant token from prior registration step
steps: Execute Flow A check-in step: POST self-checkin or staff checkins for the registered participant
Verify response before proceeding to feedback step
cases.expected: Check-in response succeeds with non-null checkinAt and CheckedIn state, enabling subsequent feedback and eligibility steps in the lifecycle flow
